exercises.title: A: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: My aunt began her career as a shop ...... before becoming the manager. (ASSIST)
The new business became very ...... after only two years. (SUCCESS)
The quick decision ...... the project from failure. (SAVE)
items.explanation: Past tense of SAVE → **saved** the project.

items.sentence: Our team leader gives advice, but she is never ...... . (BOSS)
All the ...... received extra training last month. (EMPLOY)
Teaching is a rewarding but demanding ...... . (OCCUPY)
The company made an ...... announcement about the new offices. (OFFICE)
After thirty-five years at the school, Mr Harris finally reached ...... . (RETIRE)
The designer became ...... after creating a very popular app. (FAME)
Now that she's ......, she spends more time travelling. (RETIRE)

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
The room is so noisy that I can't study. **too**
The room is ...... me to study.
items.explanation: **So** noisy → **too noisy for** me to study.

items.sentence:
I can't go to bed early because I have to finish this report. **up**
I have to ...... late to finish this report.

He is too inexperienced to lead the team. **enough**
He isn't ...... to lead the team.

The director cancelled the interview. **off**
The director ...... the interview.
